preamble: De-affrication of [dz] to [z] involves the loss of the initial stop component, common in various European languages and dialects.

examples.note: Regional variations in Italy often show de-affrication of voiced dental affricates in intervocalic positions.
Historical Slavic de-affrication occurred in various positions during the development of several daughter languages.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
